rulings.Case Title: THE AVINOAM MANDELBROT FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 19, 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Former Trustee Tzipora Sobol For Order Approving Compensation Of Trustee And Compensation Of Attorneys For Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although no proof of service is on file to show a copy of the proposed order was served on each person entitled to notice. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). The judicial officer is to review and determine whether the petition is ready for submission without requiring additional evidence or supplemental briefing on any issue, such as: 1) Should the former trustee be required to file additional evidence in support of her request for percentage-based fees for 37 months of the trust administration services from May 2021 through June 2024, equal to 0.666% per year of the trust's value as of May 2021, such as supporting time records (see LRSF 14.52) or a list of subtotal hours spent on each category of service performed to administer the trust (see CRC 7.776(6)) if the former trustee did not keep detailed time records? 2) Should the former trustee be required to segregate her services performed as an officer or director of Arik Corporation, Arik Investments LP, and Arik Management Company, from her trust administration services which should only include the trustee's passive duties as a mere investor, shareholder or limited partner of each entity (but should exclude services that should have been performed by each entity's representatives)? 3) If the former trustee performed services that should have been performed by each entity's representatives, should the former trustee's fees for those services be claimed against the entities that benefited therefrom, rather than against the trust? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE AVINOAM MANDELBROT FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 19, 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: Trustee David Mandelbrots Petition For Order: (1) To Require Former Trustee Tzipora Sobol To Reimburse The Trust For All Litigation Attorneys Fees And Costs; (2) Surcharge For Damages To The Trust; (3) Return Trust Property; (4) To Confirm Trustees Objections To Accountings; And (5) Confirming Defense Of The Purported Restatement Constitutes A Trust Contest
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following procedural issues: 1) Notice has not been given to Anshey Sfard Synagogue and Oz Mandelbrot, specific cash beneficiaries whose signed receipts required by Probate Code section 1206(b) are not on file. 2) Notice has not been given to Davia Moore, Patti Ozeri, and Larry Rosing. Although they signed receipts purporting to acknowledge full satisfaction of their cash gifts, 7% interest that started to accrue in June 2022 (one year after the settlor's death) per sections 16370(e) & 12003 does not appear to have been paid. Once the foregoing issues are cleared, the judicial officer is to review and determine whether the petition is ready for submission without requiring additional evidence or supplemental briefing on any issue, such as: i) Should Article 6.2(f)(xv) of the 2019 trust amendment/restatement be read to have authorized the former trustee to defend the 2019 trust amendment/restatement, even though Article 6.2(f)(xv) merely authorized the trustee to "prosecute or defend actions, claims, or proceedings of whatever kind for the protection of the trust property and of the trustee in the performance of the trustee's duties" (without mentioning trust contests)? ii) Did the Court's 02/06/2023 order that denied a preliminary injunction against the former trustee's use of the trust funds to defend the 2019 trust amendment/restatement necessarily determine that Article 6.2(f)(xv) should apply to trust contests in the former trustee's favor? iii) If the former trustee had no authority to use the trust funds to defend the 2019 trust amendment/restatement and is required to reimburse the trust, should the reimbursement be limited to her attorneys' litigation-related fees and costs paid from the trust without including litigation-related fees and costs paid from Arik Corporation and Arik Investments LP? If so, does the successor trustee have standing to seek reimbursement of the attorneys' fee and costs paid from the entities without joining them as necessary parties or having them file a separate civil action? iv) Was the former trustee's defense of the 2019 trust amendment/restatement a direct contest filed as a beneficiary within the meaning of sections 21310(a) and 21311(a)(1), rather than as a disinterested trustee? If so, did the former trustee have probable cause required by section 21311(b) when she filed her responsive pleading in December 2021? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) The accounting exceeds the number of years this Court permits for a first accounting. [LRSF 14.34 A.] (4) The report does not appear to include all the disclosures required by LRSF 14.35 J. It also does not address why the estate is still open. (5) Did the administrator give Notice of Proposed Action before selling the real property? (6) Please file copies of the seller's closing statements to verify the costs of the sales. (7) Who withdrew all the cash after the decedent's death? (8) The disbursements schedule does not have four columns showing the date, payee, description, and amount of each transaction. (Consumables? Legal order fees? Automobile when the estate does not own a car? Discover card payments? Meals? What "transfer" to Michelle Franklin?) (9) Did the administrator request information from the Police Credit Union regarding the very large unknown disbursement of $107,290.62 shortly after Decedent's death? (10) There is well over the FDIC insured amount at just one bank. (11) No courtesy copy of the accounting petition has been received by the examiner, but the cash on hand at the beginning of the accounting does not appear to agree with the cash shown in the I&As. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The examiner is aware that objections to the accounting were just filed on 7/15/25. No courtesy copy has been received yet; they have not been reviewed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIN YEE POON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A person's attorney may sign or verify a petition, objection or response, if the petitioner, objector or respondent is unable to do so, unless the person is a fiduciary appointed in the proceeding. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (2) The proposed distribution shown in the supplement filed on 6/9/25 does not appear to account for the $465 in attorney costs. (3) Cash is cash, regardless of where it is held. If the Fidelity account was all cash, then the cash on hand, as shown in paragraph 2 of Schedule A to the petition appears to be a more accurate characterization of the assets to be distributed. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/2/25. (Issues should just be addressed in a supplement or amendment to a petition that stands on its own and does not "answer" the issues as though taking a test.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A copy of the Fidelity Investments account statement immediately preceding the date of death was not filed, as directed in the tentative rulings that the attorney otherwise followed by answering in numbered sequence. No courtesy copy of the petition has been provided yet either. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE NELL TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption does not indicate that any IAEA authority is being requested. (2) Item 1 of the petition is incomplete (name of newspaper where notice will be published). (3) No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (4) Item 3 of the petition is incomplete if the value of the real property is estimated to be $693,689, but the net value of the real property is reportedly $400,000... without any indication of the amount of encumbrance. (5) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (6) Items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be completed if issue (a child or grandchild) survived. (7) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. (8) The children, who are the heirs, have priority before a grandchild. They must either decline to serve or nominate the petitioner. (9) The heirs have not waived bond. (10) How is the petitioner a grandchild of the decedent? Who is or was his parent? If that child of the decedent is deceased, the date of that child's death must be set forth. (11) No proof of service has been filed.
